name: GPTQ
expansion: GPT-Quantization (named after target: Generative Pre-trained Transformers)
oneliner: Post-training weight quantization using second-order (Hessian) information to compress weights to 3-4 bits with minimal accuracy loss.
explanation: Works layer-by-layer: quantizing one weight badly can be corrected by adjusting remaining weights. The Hessian (H = 2XX^T from calibration data) tells exactly how. Descends from Optimal Brain Surgeon → Optimal Brain Quantization. GPTQ's insight: for large models, greedy ordering is unnecessary — process columns left-to-right in blocks of 128. Uses Cholesky decomposition of inverse Hessian. Quantizes 175B model in ~4 GPU-hours. 128 calibration samples.
fundamentals: Objective: $\min \|WX - Q(W)X\|^2$. OBS update: when $w_q$ is quantized, distribute error via $\delta w = -\delta \cdot (H^{-1}_{:,q} / H^{-1}_{q,q})$. GPTQ processes columns left-to-right with lazy batch updates per 128-column block. Complexity: $O(d_{\text{row}} \times d_{\text{col}}^2)$.

foundational_papers.title: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-Trained Transformers
